text: The assistant describes the placement of trocars for the retroperitoneal robotic nephrectomy. The first trocar is positioned at the midpoint between the twelfth rib and the iliac border along the mid-axillary line to establish the retroperitoneal space. Two additional trocars are placed: one at the same level as the camera, at least eight centimeters away, and another in the posterior axillary line, also eight centimeters from the camera. The system trocar is positioned two centimeters anterior to the iliac border to avoid the colon.
The surgeon removes the surrounding fat tissue in the retroperitoneal space to expose the anatomical structures clearly.
The surgeon gradually enlarges the surgical space by carefully pushing away the surrounding fat tissue, revealing a clearer view of the anatomical structures.
The surgeon identifies a plane during the operation, emphasizing the importance of careful technique in this phase.
The surgeon removes the fat tissue outside Gerota's fascia to reveal clear anatomical landmarks necessary for the nephrectomy procedure.
The surgeon identifies a plane outside the surface of the kidney, preparing to approach the crossing vessel after removing surrounding fat tissue.

The surgeon opens the fat tissue and genital fascia, creating a window to access the arteriole outside the fascia. The fat tissue is visibly attached to the muscle, and the single artery appears small in size.
The surgeon isolates the renal sinus space, revealing the tumor's location. The camera captures the renal vein, highlighting its position within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses an instrument with a needle protruding from its tip, likely for precise tissue manipulation or suturing.
